q: Synaptic effects of cocaine and neonicotinoids: Which statement correctly contrasts these two chemicals?
options: Both chemicals block neurotransmitter receptors; cocaine blocks dopamine receptors and neonicotinoids block acetylcholine receptors

Cocaine blocks reuptake of monoamines (dopamine/norepinephrine) in mammalian synapses, prolonging stimulation; neonicotinoids act as nicotinic acetylcholine receptor agonists in insect nervous systems, causing continuous excitation
Neonicotinoids block acetylcholinesterase in bees; cocaine stimulates presynaptic dopamine release in humans
Both are inhibitory; cocaine increases GABA activity and neonicotinoids increase serotonin reuptake
explanation: Cocaine targets dopamine/norepinephrine reuptake transporters in mammalian brains; neonicotinoids bind and activate nicotinic ACh receptors in insects — distinct mechanisms at different receptor types in different organisms.
